title: EPG matching: index queries wrong tvg_id namespace (returns 0 programs)
description: CRITICAL — feature non-functional against real Dispatcharr. EPGProgramIndex.build() calls search_programs(tvg_id=<M3U stream tvg_id>), but /api/epg/programs/search/ filters on program.tvg_id (the EPG-source numeric id, e.g. '68344'). Candidate M3U streams carry tvg_id in a different namespace (e.g. 'CSPAN.us'). Proven live on Dispatcharr 0.24.0: search(tvg_id='CSPAN.us')=0, search(tvg_id='68344')=203. A program payload has 3 namespaces: program.tvg_id (numeric), channel.tvg_id ('CSPANHD'), stream.tvg_id ('CSPAN.us'). The spike (183.1) said join key = embedded streams[].tvg_id, but the impl feeds that value to a param that filters program.tvg_id, so every fetch returns empty -> 0 matches always. Fix needs a design decision: (a) resolve stream.tvg_id -> channel.epg_data_id -> program.tvg_id, or (b) fetch by time-window and join client-side on embedded streams[].tvg_id (breaks scope discipline), or (c) other. Candidate linear streams are not yet on Dispatcharr channels at match time (Teamarr creates channels later), complicating (a).
close_reason: Fixed in EPG matching overhaul: cascade resolver (tvg_id namespace bridge, no channel requirement), naive-datetime fix, pipe-join build_match_input, preview tvg_id. Verified live (19 matches). 685 tests pass.

title: v2.5.0 startup crash: v73 migration UNIQUE-constraint collision in channel_sort_priorities
description: GitHub #202 (@FuRy2K) — Docker startup of v2.5.0 dies with sqlite3.IntegrityError: UNIQUE constraint failed: channel_sort_priorities.sport, channel_sort_priorities.league_code immediately after 'Schema upgraded to v72'. Root cause: _migrate_v73_dedupe_milb_renamed_codes does a blanket UPDATE channel_sort_priorities SET league_code = ? WHERE league_code = ? to rename old MiLB league codes to new codes. If the user already has sort-priority rows under both the old AND the new code for the same sport, the UPDATE violates UNIQUE(sport, league_code) and aborts startup. Other tables in scalar_targets don't have UNIQUE on the league column so they were safe — channel_sort_priorities is the only one that detonates. Fix: delete-then-update pattern for UNIQUE-constrained targets (delete old rows where a colliding new row already exists for the same sport, then UPDATE survivors). Add regression test that pre-seeds both old and new codes.

title: Fix ValueError: _get_effective_team_filter returns 4-tuple but cleanup unpacks 3
description: In event_group_processor.py line 2032, _cleanup_team_filtered_channels unpacks only 3 values from _get_effective_team_filter which now returns 4 (include, exclude, mode, bypass_playoffs). This crashes ALL multi-event group processing with ValueError: too many values to unpack. Fix: unpack 4th value.

title: Separate stream-coverage from match-volume metrics
description: Match rate exceeds 100% since EPG matching went live: per-group bar divides matched RESULTS (EPG produces many per stream) by eligible STREAMS. Split into two metrics: (1) stream coverage = distinct streams matched / eligible (true 0-100%, fixes existing matched_count/streams_matched/match_rate); (2) match volume = total matched results, shown as a count via tooltip. New column match_result_count on event_groups, match_results on processing_runs.

title: Bug: deleted Dispatcharr channel group fails all channel creation (0 channels)
description: Remote user (latest dev): 'Failed to create channel ... channel_group_id: Invalid pk 35292 - object does not exist' + 0 channels. Cause: a configured static/per-league channel group was deleted in Dispatcharr, but resolve_channel_group returns static_group_id (and the pattern fallback) WITHOUT validating existence. Every channel routed to that group is rejected by Dispatcharr -> 0 channels created (matching succeeds; creation fails). Fix: dynamic_resolver tracks valid group ids from list_groups() at init (_known_group_ids, only trusted when fetch succeeded) and _validate_group_id drops a stale configured id to ungrouped (None) with a clear warning, so channels are still created. NOTE: separate from the user's other log line (their custom league regex has a duplicate (?P<league>) group — handled gracefully, returns None; that's a user config error to fix).
close_reason: dynamic_resolver tracks valid Dispatcharr group ids at init (_known_group_ids, trusted only when the list_groups fetch succeeded); _validate_group_id drops a configured static/per-league group id that no longer exists to ungrouped (None) with a clear warning, so a deleted Dispatcharr group can't fail every channel creation (0 channels). +4 tests. 775 pass.

title: Bug: Settings blank screen — localeCompare on null league/team name
description: Remote user (pre-existing, before ybt push): going to Settings yields a blank screen with 'Uncaught TypeError: Cannot read properties of null (reading localeCompare)' in an Array.sort inside a useMemo. Cause: filteredLeagues memo (Settings.tsx:1055-1059) sorts via a.sport.localeCompare/a.name.localeCompare and crashes if any cached league has a null name or sport; same unguarded pattern in StreamOrderingManager.tsx:82 (team_name.localeCompare) and the search toLowerCase guards at Settings.tsx:1051-1052. Fix: coalesce to '' before localeCompare/toLowerCase so a single null-named row can't blank the whole Settings page. (Backend follow-up: investigate why a league/team name is null.)
close_reason: Null-guarded the localeCompare/toLowerCase sorts that blanked Settings when a cached league had a null name/sport: filteredLeagues memo (Settings.tsx) sort + search, StreamOrderingManager team sort, and the shared sortLeaguesImportFirst helper (utils.ts). Coalesce to '' so one null-named row can't crash the page. Frontend builds. Root data cause (why a league/team name is null) left as a separate backend follow-up.

title: Bug: _sync_channel_settings writes non-existent managed_channels.dispatcharr_stream_id column
description: Reported on dev/2.6.0 (user logs 2026-06-06): '[LIFECYCLE] Error syncing settings for channel NHL | CAR / VGK: no such column: dispatcharr_stream_id'. service.py:1743 sets db_updates['dispatcharr_stream_id']=stream_id then update_managed_channel UPDATEs managed_channels — but that column doesn't exist on managed_channels (it's on managed_channel_streams). V1-parity legacy (e014f83, 2026-01-01): V1 stored stream id on the channel row; V2 moved membership to managed_channel_streams (written via add_stream_to_channel). The line threw on every stream-drift detection, aborting settings sync for that channel; EPG time-shared streams in 2.6.0 make drift common so it surfaced now. Fix: remove the bogus db_updates line; the Dispatcharr-side fix (update_data['streams']) is the real drift correction and DB membership is already tracked separately.
close_reason: Removed the V1-parity leftover db_updates['dispatcharr_stream_id']=stream_id at service.py:1743 — managed_channels has no such column (it's on managed_channel_streams; DB membership is written by add_stream_to_channel). The Dispatcharr-side drift fix (update_data['streams']) remains. +3 regression tests (test_managed_channel_update_columns.py) pinning the schema split. 766 tests pass.

title: Live-test EPG matching pipeline: create US SPORTS (MSX/Infinity) group, enable EPG match, verify
description: Validate the epic-183 EPG program-matching pipeline end-to-end on the dev instance (no groups currently opt in; 0 epg matches ever). Create an event group sourced from the 'US SPORTS' Dispatcharr M3U group (MSX and/or Infinity account), enable per-group epg_match_enabled, assign template + leagues, run generation, and verify: match_method='epg' streams appear, windowed (attach_at) streams created, channels created, attach/detach windows correct.
close_reason: PASSED. Created group from MSX 'US | Sports' (101 streams), enabled epg_match: 92/101 matched, 21 match_method='epg', 21 windowed streams, 16 channels, 184 programmes. Matches correct (NBC Sports Philly->Padres@Phillies, MASN/NESN/YES->correct MLB, ESPN Deportes->Knights@Hurricanes NHL, Fox Soccer Plus->Ivory Coast@France). uye window-gating verified: 21/21 in-window retained, ordering log 'window-synced 17 channels', no empty pushes, no errors. Confirms: EPG matching was simply never enabled (per-group flag off on all groups); uye/335 broke nothing. Reverted clean: disabled group -> generation cleanup deleted 16 Dispatcharr channels -> deleted group -> marked residual EPG stream rows removed. Baseline restored (epg=0, windowed=0, 27 groups).

title: XC fallback toggle not persisting in UI (combined /settings omits epg_xtream_fallback_enabled)
description: Settings -> EPG: 'Fall back to Xtream (XC) provider EPG' toggle reverts to OFF after save+reload. Root cause: the combined GET /settings handler (api/routes/settings/__init__.py get_settings) constructs EPGSettingsModel WITHOUT epg_xtream_fallback_enabled, so it falls back to the model default (False) regardless of DB. The page loads epg state from the combined endpoint (useSettings -> setEPG(settings.epg)), so the saved value (DB=1, dedicated GET /settings/epg=true) is overwritten with False on reload. Fix: pass epg_xtream_fallback_enabled through in the combined builder. Verified: DB=1, /settings/epg=true, /settings.epg=False before fix.

title: EPG attach-before buffer ignored when stream is sole source of a channel
description:
Reported: the EPG 'Attach before' buffer (epg_stream_pre_buffer_minutes) is ignored when there are no other sources for a match.

ROOT CAUSE: _create_channel (lifecycle/service.py ~1203-1212) pushes stream_ids=[stream_id] to Dispatcharr UNCONDITIONALLY at channel-creation time, never consulting attach_at. attach_at/detach_at are written to the DB row (line 1273) but the Dispatcharr channel is created with the stream already live. Channel creation is gated by event-anchored lifecycle timing (create_threshold = same_day midnight / before_event), which is independent of and usually far earlier than attach_at = program_start - pre_buffer. So a channel whose ONLY source is an out-of-window EPG-matched linear stream goes live at create-threshold instead of attach_at — the buffer is effectively ignored.

WHY 'no other sources' matters: the only passes that window-gate membership via get_ordered_stream_ids are conditional and don't reliably correct a sole out-of-window stream:
 - generation.py _apply_stream_ordering: returns early if NO ordering rules configured (line 837); only pushes for channels where a priority CHANGED (reordered_count>0, line 876); and skips empty sets via 'if ordered_ids:' (line 882) — so a channel whose sole stream is out-of-window (gated set == []) is never pushed empty, leaving the stale stream live.
 - service.py _handle_existing_channel consolidate-add (line 987) pushes the gated set, but only when a NEW stream is added to an existing channel.
When other full-life name-matched streams exist, the channel persists and these paths gate the EPG stream correctly, so the buffer appears to work — matching the report.

PROPOSED FIX:
 1. _create_channel: window-gate the initial Dispatcharr stream_ids — if the sole/primary stream has attach_at set and now is outside [attach_at, detach_at), create with stream_ids=[] (or the in-window subset) so it attaches only when the window opens.
 2. Ensure a per-run window sync pushes get_ordered_stream_ids for ALL managed channels (not just reordered ones), and ALLOW pushing an empty set (drop/relax the 'if ordered_ids:' guard) so a sole out-of-window stream is swapped out — and swapped back in when its window opens, regardless of whether ordering rules are configured.
 3. Verify Dispatcharr accepts channel creation / update with empty streams.

Needs live verification against Dispatcharr 0.24.0+ with an EPG-matched group and a single-source event.

title: EPG matching: _build_epg_index crashes on naive datetime (FIXED, uncommitted)
description: event_group_processor._build_epg_index built day_start = datetime.combine(target_date, time.min) (naive) then called to_utc(), which raises ValueError('Cannot convert naive datetime'). The to_utc calls are OUTSIDE the try/except, so the exception propagated and crashed _match_streams for any EPG-enabled group with tvg_ids. Never caught by tests (test_epg_program_index calls EPGProgramIndex.build directly with pre-formatted windows; nothing exercised _build_epg_index with a real date). FIX APPLIED (uncommitted): localize to user tz -> datetime.combine(target_date, time.min, tzinfo=get_user_timezone()). Lint + 35 EPG tests pass. Needs a regression test.
close_reason: Fixed in EPG matching overhaul: cascade resolver (tvg_id namespace bridge, no channel requirement), naive-datetime fix, pipe-join build_match_input, preview tvg_id. Verified live (19 matches). 685 tests pass.

title: [#217] TSDB: remove dead eventsround.php, repoint Unrivaled to eventsseason
description:
GH #217 (@dbelcham, v2.5.6). 'Preview stream matches' spins endlessly for leagues TSDB serves; logs show repeated HTTP 404 for eventsround.php then 429 rate limiting.

ROOT CAUSE: eventsround.php is a niche/premium-ish endpoint (not in TSDB v1 free docs; 404s in Postman) that only works for a few sparse leagues (Unrivaled, added in commit 1d196d9). But provider.py uses it as an UNCONDITIONAL final fallback in get_events() (line ~123) and _get_events_for_team() (line ~219) whenever eventsday.php + eventsnextleague.php return no events. For a league like CFL (off-season in late May -> empty eventsday per date), every date in the ~14-day scan falls through to eventsround.php -> 404. Two amplifiers: (1) client _request retries deterministic 404s (client.py:355-357); (2) get_events_by_round only caches TRUTHY results (client.py:511), so a 404/None is NOT negative-cached and re-fires for every date. Result: dozens of 404s -> trips TSDB 429 limiter -> exponential backoff (5/10/20/40/80s) -> preview never resolves.

FIX PLAN (layered, NOT yet implemented):
1. PRIMARY — gate eventsround behind a league allowlist. Only attempt the round fallback for leagues that actually need it (Unrivaled + any verified sparse league). Skip entirely for all others. Find/define the allowlist source (Unrivaled is the only known consumer today). Eliminates the storm at the source.
2. CLIENT ROBUSTNESS — short-circuit retries on 404 in _request(): a 404 is deterministic, return None immediately instead of looping _retry_count times. Helps any endpoint.
3. DEFENSE IN DEPTH — negative-cache empty/None eventsround results per (league, season) for a TTL so allowlisted-but-empty leagues don't re-hit per date.
4. OPTIONAL UX — guard the preview path so provider backoff can't hang it for minutes; return partial/empty with a warning instead of an infinite spinner.

VERIFY: repro CFL event-group preview -> no eventsround 404s, preview returns promptly; regression-check Unrivaled still resolves via eventsround; pytest tests/ (TSDB). Relates to closed #167 (which removed an EARLIER eventsround misuse; this is a re-introduction via the Unrivaled fallback path).
notes:
FINAL PLAN (decided 2026-05-30 — KEEP Unrivaled, repoint to eventsseason):
NEW EVIDENCE: Unrivaled is a WORKING league — eventsseason.php?id=5622&s=2026 returns 56 events; eventspastleague shows real games (Lunar Owls BC, Feb 2026). It only looked dead because (a) eventsround.php is removed at TSDB and (b) it's off-season in late May. So DO NOT delete Unrivaled.

IMPLEMENTATION:
1. Replace client.get_events_by_round() (client.py:480, eventsround.php — DEAD) with get_events_by_season() using eventsseason.php?id=<league_id>&s=<season>. Same 'fetch full set -> filter by date' pattern. Season auto-detect = calendar year (Unrivaled is calendar-year; current behavior). Cache truthy results (already does).
2. Update both fallback call sites in provider.py (get_events ~123, _get_events_for_team ~219) to call the season method.
3. GATING: do NOT make eventsseason a blanket per-date fallback for every league (full-season payload per date is heavy). Gate it to leagues that need season-based fetch — prefer a per-league flag/attribute in the leagues config (Unrivaled is the only known consumer today) over a hardcoded set. Avoids both the old 404 storm AND a new heavy-fetch storm.
4. CLIENT HYGIENE: short-circuit retries on deterministic 404 in _request() (client.py:355) — return None immediately.
5. DOCS: update eventsround references -> eventsseason in docs/reference/providers/tsdb.md (lines 36/59/74 region), tsdb-api.md, and the provider.py/client.py docstrings. Unrivaled STAYS in supported-leagues (no league-count change).
VERIFY: CFL event-group preview no longer hangs (no 404 storm); Unrivaled resolves via eventsseason (in-season returns games; off-season returns scheduled/empty cleanly, no hang); pytest tests/ TSDB.
NOTE: Unrivaled is in schema.sql but missing from this dev DB — a restart/reconcile reseeds it from schema; not part of this fix.
close_reason: Fixed on dev 3050ac7: removed dead eventsround.php, repointed sparse-league fallback to eventsseason.php (gated to SEASON_FALLBACK_LEAGUES), fail-fast on 404s. 607 tests pass (+3 regression). Unrivaled preserved. GH #217 commented; close issue at v2.5.7 release.

title: CDVR: also refresh XMLTV lineup so EPG actually updates
description: User reports M3U refresh hook (#fca8704) triggers but EPG isn't actually refreshing in CDVR. Their working script does TWO calls: POST /providers/m3u/sources/{name}/refresh (channels) AND PUT /dvr/lineups/XMLTV-{name} (EPG). We only do the first. Fix: discover lineup via GET /dvr/lineups (matching by source/name convention XMLTV-{source}), then PUT /dvr/lineups/{lineup_id} after the M3U refresh. Surface failures independently.

title: Fix Channels DVR source picker — wrong endpoint causes 404
description: UI shows 'Couldn't load sources: Client error 404 Not Found' even though the connection test passes. ChannelsDVRClient.list_m3u_sources() calls GET /providers/m3u/sources, which doesn't exist on a real Channels DVR server. The actual list of M3U sources lives at GET /devices filtered by Provider=='m3u'; FriendlyName is the source name used in /providers/m3u/sources/<name>/refresh. Verified locally against Channels DVR v2026.04.29.0232. Test connection works because it hits /status. Fix: change list_m3u_sources() to use /devices + filter; update tests to match real API shape.
notes: Two bugs fixed: (1) list_m3u_sources() called nonexistent /providers/m3u/sources — now uses /devices filtered by Provider=='m3u', returning FriendlyName. (2) trigger_m3u_refresh() used PUT (404) — now uses POST (200 with body true). Verified end-to-end against live Channels DVR v2026.02.09.1530. Help text in Settings.tsx updated to reflect the new endpoint.

title: M3U-sourced channel group not displayed after page reload
description:
User reports that picking a channel group sourced from an M3U account (with the 'Show M3U-sourced channel groups' toggle on) appears to lose the value after leaving the settings page and returning.

Root cause: the toggle is local React state defaulting to false, so on page reload the channelGroupsQuery refetches with exclude_m3u=true. The saved channel-group ID is filtered out of the option list. The native <select> falls back to the default option ('None' / 'Default (inherit)') even though the underlying React state still holds the saved value.

Affected pickers (both in Settings.tsx, both feed off channelGroupsQuery):
- Default Channel Group (Dispatcharr tab)
- Per-league channel group override (LeagueConfigRow)

Bug introduced by b602e62 (toggle for M3U-sourced channel groups).

Fix:
1. Backend /channel-groups: include from_m3u: bool on each item so the frontend can filter without losing data
2. Frontend: always fetch with exclude_m3u=false; filter displayed options in JS via the toggle, but ALWAYS include the currently-selected group regardless of filter
3. Apply the same 'always include selected' rule to per-league rows

title: Bug: {home_team_short}/{away_team_short} resolve blank for MLB events (#201)
description:
@scoobwrx reports on 2.4.5-dev+4f9b411 that {home_team_short} and {away_team_short} render as empty strings for MLB events in event template channel names.

Variables exist and are wired (home_away.py:138/150) — they read game_ctx.event.away_team.short_name directly. Root cause is upstream: the MLB event-time team object likely has short_name='' or None.

Investigation steps:
1. Reproduce: regenerate EPG with MLB matched events using the variables.
2. Check what populates event.home_team / event.away_team for MLB events — ESPN provider for MLB? MLBStats provider only handles MiLB.
3. Verify ESPN provider's _build_event_team or equivalent populates short_name from shortDisplayName for both home and away teams in the events scoreboard response (provider.py:357 sets it for one path).
4. Fix at the populator layer so MLB event teams carry short_name end-to-end.
5. Add a regression test asserting short_name is non-empty after parse for ESPN MLB events.
notes:
Round 2 diagnosis (log generate-dev+6d32743.log): user IS on the fixed build (2.4.5-dev+6d32743). My self-heal predicate didn't fire (no [CACHE_STALE] lines, only the pre-existing date-based MATCH_CACHE Stale). The broken channel names on disk persist because all the user's MLB events at generation time were status=final and got EXCLUDED → EVENT_FINAL by the lifecycle. EXCLUDED events bypass _sync_channel_settings, so the name-drift detection at service.py:1584 never runs to re-render them. So even if cached event data is now correct, the existing managed_channels rows keep their old broken names until the lifecycle deletes them.

Two real fixes possible:
(a) One-shot migration: scan managed_channels for names that look broken (e.g. starts with ' at ', contains double spaces from empty {*_short}), regenerate from current event cache.
(b) Always run the name-correction half of _sync_channel_settings even when the event is EVENT_FINAL — separate the 'should we touch this channel for lifecycle reasons' question from 'is the rendered name still correct'.

User volunteered to wipe cache/config and start fresh; I greenlit that as the immediate fix path. If a clean start works, no further code change needed. If it doesn't, (a) or (b) becomes mandatory.
close_reason: Real fix on dev (3 layers): (1) refresh_event_status is now additive — overlays only mutable fields (status, scores, broadcasts, odds, fight_result_*) onto the original event. Teams and other static fields are preserved verbatim. ESPN's summary endpoint omits shortDisplayName, so the old code was wiping team.short_name=None over the populated scoreboard data on every refresh. (2) Service-layer backfill from team_cache: get_event/get_events/get_team_schedule walk teams and patch short_name/abbreviation/name from team_cache when missing. team_cache (refreshed on startup from /teams endpoints) is now the canonical identity source, decoupled from per-event endpoint shape. (3) ESPN _parse_team falls back to team.name (the short form, always populated) when shortDisplayName is missing — defense in depth for new endpoints. 17 new tests, 548 total pass.

title: Fix duplicate MiLB leagues in selector (orphaned rows from v2.2 rename)
description: Commit db53687 (Apr 13, 2026) renamed MiLB league_codes (a→milb-a, aa→milb-aa, aaa→milb-aaa, higha→milb-high-a). schema.sql uses INSERT OR REPLACE keyed on league_code, so the new rows were inserted but old rows never deleted. Result: duplicate entries in the league selector. User data also needs updating: sports_subscription JSON arrays, managed_channels.league (5 aaa + 3 higha), team_cache (30 teams under each old code, duplicates of new). Need migration v73 to delete orphaned league/team_cache rows AND remap user-data references.
close_reason: v73 migration deletes orphan MiLB league rows (a, aa, aaa, higha) and remaps user-data references to new codes; tests added; verified on copy of prod DB

title: Filler programmes emit to base event channel instead of feed-separated channel (v2.4.4 regression)
description:
Reported in Discord by remote user via ChatGPT log analysis. Pregame filler programmes are written with the BASE event channel ID (`teamarr-event-{event_id}`) while the actual live game programmes are correctly written to the feed-separated channel IDs (`teamarr-event-{event_id}-feed-{team_id}`). Result: when feed separation is active, users see the live game on a child channel with no filler, and the filler appears on a parent channel with no live programme. Only games without feed separation (e.g., national broadcasts like CIN @ PIT) work correctly because both filler and game land on the same base channel.

## Root cause
`event_group_processor.py:2506` calls `generate_event_tvg_id(event.id, event.provider, segment, exception_keyword)` without the optional `feed_team_id` parameter. The matching equivalent in `event_epg.py:407` correctly passes `feed_team_id`. Bug introduced in v2.4.4 commit `98a2ef7` which threaded feed_team_id through the EPG path but missed the filler path.

## Fix
Read `feed_team` from `stream_match` (already populated by `_resolve_feed_teams` at line 991) and pass its id to `generate_event_tvg_id`. One-line addition mirroring `event_epg.py:405-408`.

## Test
Add a test that exercises `_generate_filler_for_streams` with a `stream_match` containing `feed_team` and asserts the channel_id includes the `-feed-{id}` suffix.

title: Bug 2: feed-label auto-append duplicates when template uses {feed_team} variables
description: Root cause: _generate_channel_name() in teamarr/consumers/lifecycle/service.py:1292 unconditionally appends '(Pirates Feed)' when feed_team and feed_label_style are set. Users using {feed_team}/{feed_team_short}/{feed_team_abbrev} etc. in their channel-name template get duplication like 'Pirates @ Phillies - Pirates (Pirates)'. The same anti-duplication pattern already exists for {exception_keyword} on line 1275 (template_uses_keyword check) but isn't applied to feed vars. Fix: define FEED_TEMPLATE_VARS set, check if any are present in name_format before auto-appending.
acceptance_criteria: Channel-name template containing any {feed_team*} variable does not get the auto-append suffix. Templates without those vars still get the auto-append. Tests cover both paths.

title: Bug 1: tvg_id collision causes feed-separated channels to share EPG
description: Root cause: generate_event_tvg_id() in teamarr/consumers/lifecycle/types.py:135 doesn't include feed_team_id. When feed separation creates 3 channels for one event (HOME/AWAY/National), all 3 generate the same tvg_id like 'teamarr-event-401547679'. In XMLTV all 3 programmes share that channel ID, so Dispatcharr displays whichever programme parses first across all channels — typically the home-team match. managed_channels already discriminates by feed_team_id (schema v69 unique index in connection.py:1322), but tvg_id doesn't — that's the asymmetry. Fix: add feed_team_id parameter to generate_event_tvg_id() and slug-append it. Thread through 3 callsites: event_epg.py:405, lifecycle/service.py:1051, lifecycle/service.py:1632. No schema migration — drift detection regenerates existing channels' tvg_ids on next sync.
acceptance_criteria: Three feed-separated channels for the same event get distinct tvg_ids. Existing feed-separated channels migrate via drift detection on next sync. Tests verify uniqueness.

title: Feed-separated channels share EPG + auto-append duplicates with template vars
description: Two related feed-separation bugs reported in chat (mz83 in #teamarr-chat, 4/23). Channels are correctly named per-feed via the auto-append, but the EPG title/description is wrong, AND users who use {feed_team} template vars in their channel-name template get duplicated feed labels because the auto-append still fires.

title: Scope template variables by template_type (team vs event) at the registry level
description:
## Problem

The template_type concept ('team' vs 'event') exists throughout the codebase — schema (templates.template_type CHECK IN team/event), DB filter (templates.py:275), UI banner (TemplateForm.tsx:660 says 'Showing vars for: Team/Event'), conditions API (variables.py:110 scopes conditions) — EXCEPT variables themselves. GET /variables returns all 205 variables regardless of template_type, and VariableSidebar promises scoping it doesn't deliver.

This lets users put team-perspective variables (like {team}, {opponent}, {is_home}, {team_record}, {win_streak}) into event templates, where they silently render from the home_team perspective (event_epg.py:411 hardcodes team_id=event.home_team.id). Cosmetically identical to {home_team}, redundant, misleading.

The inverse is also true: feed_team variables ({feed_team}, {is_home_feed}, etc.) appear in team-template pickers where they resolve to empty because ctx.feed_team is never set on team channels.

PR #196 tried to patch one symptom — making team_id resolve to the feed_team on feed-separated event channels — but that perpetuates the design bug. Correct fix: scope the variables at the registry level so event templates don't offer {team}/{opponent}/{is_home}/etc. in the first place, and team templates don't offer feed_team vars.

## Plan

### 1. Registry infrastructure (teamarr/templates/variables/registry.py)
- Add ChannelScope enum: BOTH (default), TEAM_ONLY, EVENT_ONLY
- Add channel_scope: ChannelScope = ChannelScope.BOTH to VariableDefinition
- Update @register_variable decorator to accept channel_scope
- Add VariableRegistry.filter_by_scope(template_type: str) → Iterator[VariableDefinition]
  - 'team' → BOTH + TEAM_ONLY
  - 'event' → BOTH + EVENT_ONLY
  - None/other → all

### 2. Classify variables
Team-only (perspective-dependent):
  - identity.py: team, team_short, team_abbrev, team_abbrev_lower, team_pascal, team_logo; opponent_*; matchup, matchup_short
  - home_away.py: is_home, is_away, home_away_text, vs_at, vs_@
  - records.py: team_record, opponent_record
  - streaks.py: team-oriented streaks
  - scores.py: team_score, opponent_score
  - outcome.py: result, result_text, etc.
  - standings.py: playoff_seed, games_back
  - statistics.py: team_*, opponent_*
  - rankings.py: team_rank, is_ranked, opponent_rank, is_ranked_opponent
  - conference.py: team/opponent conference/division variables

Event-only:
  - home_away.py: feed_team, feed_team_short, feed_team_abbrev, feed_team_abbrev_lower, feed_team_logo, is_home_feed, is_away_feed, feed_home_away (all 8)

BOTH (default — positional or game-level):
  - identity: league, sport, league_short, exception_keyword
  - datetime: all game_date/game_time etc.
  - venue: all venue_*
  - home_away: home_team, away_team, home_team_short, etc. (positional)
  - records: home_record, away_record (if exist)
  - scores: home_score, away_score, final_score
  - playoffs: season_type, is_playoff, is_preseason, is_regular_season
  - odds: all (event-level)
  - broadcast: all
  - soccer: all (positional soccer vars)
  - combat: all (event-level combat sports)

### 3. API scoping (teamarr/api/routes/variables.py)
- GET /variables accepts optional template_type: str = None query param
- When set to 'team' or 'event', filter registry by scope
- When not set, return all (backward compat for any external consumer)

### 4. Frontend wire-up
- frontend/src/api/variables.ts: fetchVariables(templateType?: string)
- frontend/src/pages/TemplateForm.tsx: pass isTeamTemplate ? 'team' : 'event' into the query key AND the fetch call
- The 'Showing vars for:' banner becomes truthful

### 5. Render-time behavior (deferred — no change in this bead)
Existing event templates with hand-typed {team} continue to render as home_team (current behavior). Picker scoping prevents NEW misuse; existing templates not disturbed. A future bead can add render-time rejection if desired.

### 6. Tests
- tests/test_variable_scope.py
  - ChannelScope enum + filter_by_scope behavior
  - GET /variables?template_type=team excludes feed_team* variables
  - GET /variables?template_type=event excludes team/opponent/is_home/team_record/etc.
  - GET /variables (no param) returns all 205
  - Scope is preserved through the response (frontend sees which vars it got)

### 7. Docs
- docs/guide/templates/variables.md: note that some variables only appear in team or event contexts
- docs/reference/architecture/template-engine.md: document ChannelScope alongside SuffixRules

## Consequences
- PR #196 (Fix feed_team always resolving from home team perspective) becomes unnecessary — the {team}/{opponent}/{is_home} variables it fixes for event templates shouldn't be accessible there at all
- PR #196 'National' fallback for feed_team is explicitly rejected — {feed_team} should render empty when no feed is assigned (graceful disappear preserved)
- Existing event templates that use {team} continue rendering home_team for backward compat; users migrate at their own pace using the (now correctly scoped) picker

## Origin
Support chat investigation 2026-04-22. User feedback: team/opponent shouldn't be available on event templates; feed_team shouldn't be available on team templates. Existing UI banner promises scoping but API doesn't deliver.

Closes: will supersede PR #196.

title: Canonicalize season_type across providers + fix playoff bypass (closes #197 root cause)
description:
## Problem

The playoff bypass feature ('Include all playoff games') is silently broken for some providers and strict-equality checks make it fragile.

### Producer/consumer map (audit 2026-04-22)

**Producers:**
- ESPN: writes canonical strings ('preseason'/'regular'/'postseason'/'offseason') — providers/espn/provider.py:506 via _parse_season_type()
- MLBStats: writes raw MLB StatsAPI gameType codes ('R'/'P'/'F'/'D'/'L'/'W'/'E'/'S'/'A') — providers/mlbstats/provider.py:224
- HockeyTech: never sets it — always None
- TSDB: never sets it — always None

**Consumers:**
- Event group filter bypass (event_group_processor.py:1663) — strict '== postseason' — fails for MLBStats/HockeyTech/TSDB
- {is_playoff} template var (templates/variables/playoffs.py:38) — fuzzy 'post' OR 'playoff' — fails for MLBStats single-letter codes
- {is_preseason} template var — fuzzy 'pre' OR 'exhibition' — fails for MLBStats E/S
- {is_regular_season} — accidentally works for MLBStats R (negation path lands correctly)

**Additional gap found during audit:**
- ESPN summary endpoint response (used by refresh_event_status during _enrich_matched_events) may not include season.type at the top level — needs verification. If it doesn't, the refresh wipes season_type to None mid-pipeline even for ESPN sources. This is the likely root cause of #197.

### Affected surfaces
- MiLB playoffs (MLBStats): bypass and is_playoff silently fail
- HockeyTech sports (CHL/AHL/ECHL/PWHL/USHL) playoffs: no bypass possible
- TSDB sports (AFL/NRL/Super Rugby/cricket/boxing) playoffs: no bypass possible
- Possibly ESPN playoffs after refresh_event_status if summary response lacks season.type

## Plan (Option A — producer-side canonicalization)

### 1. Canonical enum
Define canonical values in core/types.py as module-level constants:
  SEASON_POSTSEASON = 'postseason'
  SEASON_REGULAR = 'regular'
  SEASON_PRESEASON = 'preseason'
  SEASON_OFFSEASON = 'offseason'

### 2. Producer normalization
- ESPN: already canonical, no change needed
- MLBStats: add mapping in providers/mlbstats/provider.py
    gameType -> canonical:
      'R' -> 'regular'
      'S' -> 'preseason'  (Spring Training)
      'E' -> 'preseason'  (Exhibition)
      'F', 'D', 'L', 'W', 'P' -> 'postseason'  (five playoff series codes)
      'A' -> None  (All Star — not a season-type)
      other/unknown -> None
- HockeyTech: investigate if API exposes a playoff/regular flag. Add mapping if yes; document None if no.
- TSDB: investigate the same. Add mapping if yes.

### 3. Verify ESPN summary endpoint preserves season_type
- Check providers/espn/provider.py get_event() / summary parsing path
- If summary response omits season.type at top level, preserve it from the caller's event (similar to the segment_times preservation in services/sports_data.py:278-281)

### 4. Consumer alignment
- event_group_processor.py:1663: change strict == to use SEASON_POSTSEASON constant (or a helper is_postseason())
- templates/variables/playoffs.py: rewrite is_playoff/is_preseason/is_regular_season to compare against canonical constants, not fuzzy substring match

### 5. Migration
- No schema change needed (season_type is just a TEXT column)
- Cached events with old MLBStats codes will naturally expire (cache TTL) or can be wiped via the existing Cache Clear UI
- Document in release notes that users on MiLB/HockeyTech/TSDB who want playoff bypass to work should hit Cache Refresh once

### 6. Tests (critical — feature is currently untested)
- Add tests/test_playoff_bypass.py
- Cover: global on + group null + non-subscribed postseason event -> included
- Cover: global off -> excluded
- Cover: group override True trumps global False
- Cover: group override False trumps global True
- Cover: season_type None -> normal filter applies
- Cover: MLBStats canonical mapping (P/F/D/L/W -> postseason)
- Cover: is_playoff, is_preseason, is_regular_season template vars against all four canonical values

### 7. Docs
- docs/reference/providers/mlbstats.md: document gameType mapping
- docs/reference/providers/espn.md: document canonical season_type values
- docs/guide/templates/variables.md: clarify season_type variable values (postseason/regular/preseason/offseason)

## Verification
- Before shipping, confirm root cause of #197 by reproducing with an Event Group + team filter + bypass enabled against a current NHL playoff game
- If root cause was the summary-endpoint gap, confirm fix resolves it
- If root cause was something else, leave #197 open pending further investigation

## Scope / NOT in this bead
- Fixing 'team_filter_enabled' master toggle (separate bead, separate issue)
- Moving/renaming the UI label (deferred pending user feedback)

title: Event-Anchored Channel Lifecycle Overhaul
description:
Channel lifecycle timing is anchored to day boundaries (midnight, end-of-day 23:59) and stream existence. This causes: (1) Midnight crossover: events ending after midnight get scheduled_delete_at = 23:59 of the end date — channels sit stale for ~23 hours. (2) Persistent stream blindness: M3U providers reuse stream IDs/names for different events. cleanup_deleted_streams can't detect content rotation when names stay the same. (3) Multi-stream survival: in consolidation, if one stream keeps its name, the channel survives even when other streams rotated.

Solution: Two coupled changes — Part 1: Streamline timing options to two create modes (same_day, before_event) + two delete modes (same_day, after_event), fix midnight crossover, add event-anchored option with configurable buffers. Part 2: Event-aware stream cleanup — validate streams against matched event data, remove streams that rotated to different events, delete channel when last stream is gone.

title: Generation reliability: network resilience & EPG refresh dedup
Sports Subscription — Decouple sports from event groups
description: Replace per-group sport/league/template configuration with a single global Sports Subscription. Groups become sport-agnostic stream suppliers. Remove parent-child hierarchy. All groups processed through unified multi-league code path. See plan transcript for full details.

title: Gold Zone: managed channel registration + EPG association + profile settings
description: Gold Zone channel must be registered as a managed channel so EPG association works through the standard pipeline. Also need to ensure Gold Zone EPG appears in /groups/xmltv/combined endpoint (where Dispatcharr reads from), and add channel group/profile/stream profile settings with shared UI components.

title: Fix Gold Zone: filter stale streams, scope to event groups, fix EPG merge, datetime disambiguation
Add tests for keyword-aware tvg-id and EPG generation
description:
Tests to cover:
1. generate_event_tvg_id with exception_keyword produces correct format
2. Keyword slugification (spaces, special chars, unicode)
3. Two channels for same event with different keywords get different tvg-ids
4. EPG programme entries use the correct keyword-specific tvg-id
5. {exception_keyword} resolves in title/subtitle/description templates
6. Channels without keywords are unaffected (backward compatible)

title: Handle tvg-id migration for existing channels
description:
Existing channels with exception keywords have old-format tvg-ids (without the keyword suffix). When upgrading:

1. On first run after upgrade, channels with exception_keyword set need their tvg_id updated in the database
2. Dispatcharr channels need their tvg-id updated to match
3. Consider doing this lazily during the next generation cycle rather than a migration — when lifecycle service touches a channel, regenerate its tvg-id with the keyword

This avoids a schema migration and handles it gracefully during normal operation.

title: Inject exception_keyword into EPG programme generation
description:
Update event_epg.py so that when generating XMLTV programmes for a channel with an exception keyword:

1. The programme's channel_id uses the keyword-aware tvg-id (matching the channel)
2. The exception_keyword is injected into extra_vars for template resolution
3. {exception_keyword} resolves correctly in title, subtitle, and description templates — not just channel_name_format

Currently event_epg.py lines 412-426 only handle {exception_keyword} for channel names via string replacement. The template resolver needs the keyword in extra_vars to resolve it in other template fields.

This is the key change that makes exception keywords useful in EPG output.

title: Pass exception_keyword through channel creation to tvg-id
description:
Update lifecycle/service.py channel creation flow to pass the matched exception_keyword to generate_event_tvg_id(). Currently line 1044 calls generate_event_tvg_id(event_id, event_provider, segment) without the keyword.

Also update find_existing_channel / channel deduplication to account for the new tvg-id format — channels with different keywords already have different rows (UNIQUE constraint includes exception_keyword), but now their tvg-ids will also differ.

Ensure Dispatcharr sync uses the new keyword-aware tvg-id when creating/updating channels.

title: Extend generate_event_tvg_id to accept exception_keyword
description:
Modify generate_event_tvg_id() in teamarr/consumers/lifecycle/types.py to accept an optional exception_keyword parameter. When provided, append a sanitized slug of the keyword to the tvg-id.

Current signature: generate_event_tvg_id(event_id, provider, segment)
New signature: generate_event_tvg_id(event_id, provider, segment, exception_keyword)

Output examples:
- No keyword: teamarr-event-401547679
- With 'Spanish': teamarr-event-401547679-spanish
- With segment + keyword: teamarr-event-401547679-prelims-spanish

Sanitize keyword to lowercase alphanumeric slug (replace spaces with hyphens, strip special chars).

title: Exception keyword EPG uniqueness
description:
Exception keywords currently only affect channel names but NOT EPG programme data. All channels for the same event share the same tvg-id (teamarr-event-{event_id}), so they share identical XMLTV <programme> entries. This means {exception_keyword} in title/subtitle/description templates resolves to empty string.

Fix: Make tvg-id unique per exception keyword so each variant gets its own XMLTV channel and programme entries, allowing the template engine to correctly resolve {exception_keyword} in all template fields (not just channel_name_format).

Current: teamarr-event-{event_id}[-{segment}]
Proposed: teamarr-event-{event_id}[-{segment}][-{keyword}]

Example: teamarr-event-401547679-spanish

Affected areas:
- generate_event_tvg_id() in lifecycle/types.py
- Channel creation in lifecycle/service.py
- EPG programme generation in event_epg.py
- Template resolution (extra_vars injection of exception_keyword)
- Dispatcharr channel sync (tvg-id used as channel identifier)

title: Validate template modal with Playwright
description: Use Playwright browser automation to validate the redesigned template assignment modal. Test scenarios: single group template edit, bulk edit with multiple groups, bulk import template assignment. Verify sport/league pickers filter correctly, multi-select works, and assignments save properly.

title: Redesign leagues picker as searchable multi-select dropdown
description: Replace the SearchableMultiSelect leagues picker with a scrollable list matching the LeaguePicker pattern. Features: free text filter input, checkbox multi-select, select-all/clear-all buttons, sport-grouped display. Should show league names with checkboxes in a scrollable container, filtered by search text.

title: Redesign sports picker as searchable multi-select dropdown
description: Replace the badge-toggle sports selector in TemplateAssignmentModal with a searchable dropdown similar to LeaguePicker. Features: free text filter, checkboxes with multi-select, select-all/clear-all. Should match the dual-dropdown pattern used in league selection for multi-league groups.

title: Fix bulk template assignment toolbar button (#131)
description: The toolbar template button in EventGroups.tsx (line ~1059) always sets templateAssignmentGroupId to the first selected group instead of using templateAssignmentBulkIds when multiple groups are selected. Fix to use bulk mode when selectedIds.size > 1.

title: Template Assignment Modal UI Overhaul
description:
Redesign the template assignment modal sport/league pickers to use dual dropdown pattern with free text filter and multi-select, inspired by LeaguePicker. Fix bug #131 (bulk edit only updates first group). Ensure same modal works for single group edit, bulk edit, and bulk import.

Related: GitHub #131, #102

title: Bulk Template Assignments for Multi-League Groups
description:
**Problem:** When using bulk edit on multi-league groups, the 'Manage Templates' button only opens the template assignment modal for the first selected group. Users cannot apply the same template configuration to multiple groups at once.

**Solution:** Add bulk mode to TemplateAssignmentModal that applies template assignments to all selected groups.

**Implementation:**
1. Backend: New PUT /api/v1/groups/bulk-templates endpoint
2. Frontend API: New bulkSetGroupTemplates function
3. TemplateAssignmentModal: Add bulkGroupIds prop and bulk mode UI
4. EventGroups.tsx: Pass bulk IDs instead of single ID from bulk edit

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- User can select multiple multi-league groups
- Click 'Manage Templates' opens modal in bulk mode
- User configures template assignments (start fresh)
- 'Apply to All' replaces assignments on all selected groups
- Warning text explains this replaces existing assignments

title: Cannot set 'use default channel profile' on existing groups
close_reason: Fixed: channel_profile_ids was being converted from null to [] breaking 'use default' distinction. Also fixed bulk edit Apply button disabled for Stream Profile.

title: Dispatcharr logo cleanup setting disables itself
close_reason: Fixed: cleanup_unused_logos and default_stream_profile_id missing from /api/v1/settings endpoint

title: Replace substring matching with fuzzy fighter matching
description:
**Problem**: Strategy 2 (fighter name matching) uses lax substring matching that can cause false positives:
```python
# Current (dangerous):
if len(last_name) >= 4 and last_name in stream_lower:
    return MATCHED
```

**Example false positive**: If a UFC fighter is named "John Cage", stream "CAGE WARRIORS 199" would incorrectly match.

**Root cause**: The classifier already extracts fighters using separator detection (vs/@/v), storing them in `classified.team1` and `classified.team2`. But event_matcher ignores these and does naive substring matching.

**Fix**: 
1. Only attempt Strategy 2 if fighters were extracted during classification (`classified.team1` or `classified.team2` is not None)
2. Use `rapidfuzz.fuzz.token_set_ratio` (same as team_vs_team) instead of substring matching
3. Apply confidence threshold (e.g., 75) to avoid false positives

**Benefits**:
- Streams without fighter separators ("UFC 00 : UAE WARRIORS 67") won't falsely match
- Streams with fighters ("Volkanovski vs Lopes") will properly fuzzy match
- Consistent approach with team_vs_team matcher

**Location**: `teamarr/consumers/matching/event_matcher.py` - `_match_to_event_card()` method, Strategy 2 section (lines 235-263)

title: Add max distance threshold to time-based segment detection
description:
**Problem**: `determine_segment_from_time()` matches ANY extracted time to closest segment, even with 960+ minute distances.

**Evidence from logs**:
```
[UFC_SEGMENTS] Determined segment 'early_prelims' from time 00:00:00 (tz=user, dist=960 min)
```

**Fix**: 
1. Add `MAX_SEGMENT_TIME_DISTANCE = 60` constant (60 minutes)
2. In `determine_segment_from_time()`, after finding best_segment:
   ```python
   if best_distance > MAX_SEGMENT_TIME_DISTANCE * 60:
       logger.debug("Time %s too far from any segment (dist=%d min), ignoring", stream_time, best_distance // 60)
       return None  # Falls back to main_card in caller
   ```

**Location**: `teamarr/consumers/ufc_segments.py` - `determine_segment_from_time()` function (lines 304-323)

title: Fix UFC event date/number matching
description:
**Problem**: Streams match to wrong UFC event numbers (e.g., UFC 324 instead of UFC 325 on 1/31/2026).

**Root Cause**:
- Strategy 1 in event_matcher.py (lines 202-220) uses substring matching: `event_num.lower() in event.name.lower()`
- No validation that matched event is on the target_date
- If ESPN returns both current and past events, first match wins

**Fix Needed**:
1. After event number match, validate that event.start_time is on target_date
2. If no event on target_date has matching number, return NO_MATCH
3. Log warning if event number matched but date didn't

**Files**:
- teamarr/consumers/matching/event_matcher.py (Strategy 1, add date validation)

title: Fix EVENT_CARD over-classification for UFC content
description:
**Problem**: Streams like 'UFC Fight Pass 24/7', 'UAE Warriors', 'BJJ Clubes', 'Cage Warriors' are matching to UFC 325 even though they don't have:
1. Event number (UFC 325)
2. Fighter names (Gaethje, Pimblett)
3. Correct date/time

**Root Cause**: event_matcher.py Strategy 2 (lines 222-269) matches ANY stream containing generic keywords to the only event on that date:
```python
if keyword_matches:  # Contains 'ufc', 'prelims', etc.
    if len(events) == 1:  # Only one UFC event today
        return MatchOutcome.matched(...)  # MATCH - no validation!
```

**The Bug**: Generic keywords (ufc, mma, prelims, main card) are meant for CLASSIFICATION (determining EVENT_CARD type), NOT for MATCHING to a specific event.

**Fix Needed**:
1. REMOVE Strategy 2 entirely from event_matcher.py
2. Only match if: event number OR fighter names OR time-based match
3. Streams without valid match criteria should FAIL, not match on generic keywords

**Segment Assignment** (separate from matching):
- Segment keyword detected → use that segment
- No keyword but time found → disambiguate by time
- No keyword AND no time → fallback to main_card

**Files**:
- teamarr/consumers/matching/event_matcher.py (remove Strategy 2 lines 222-269)

title: English Football League matching failures
Stream Profile Selection
description:
Allow setting Dispatcharr stream profiles at global and per-group levels. Stream profiles are fetched from Dispatcharr (no creation in Teamarr). When creating channels, pass stream_profile_id to Dispatcharr API. Closes: GitHub #99

Key findings from Dispatcharr API:
- GET /api/core/streamprofiles/ - List available profiles (ffmpeg, Proxy, Redirect, streamlink, VLC, etc.)
- Channel has stream_profile_id field (nullable int)
- Pass stream_profile_id in channel create/update payload

title: ③ EPG home — Matching/tuning zone (detection, regex, program matching, exception keywords)
description: Step 3 EPG home — MATCHING zone (secondary/'tuning'). Surface the orphaned Detection Library here; per-group regex; EPG program matching (pull global tuning out of Settings); exception keywords (DEDUP to one home). This is the troubleshooting side users touch when a match fails — kept visually secondary to the presentation/templates zone (vhbo.4) so the happy path stays clean. Same EPG nav home, separate zone.

title: Docs rewrite for new IA + Playwright e2e verification
description: Rewrite docs/guide navigation to match the 4-step IA (every settings/*.md page moved content needs its doc home updated); validate old-URL redirects; Playwright pass over each step flow end-to-end on :5173. Final phase.

title: First-run onboarding: guided 4-step setup checklist
description: First-run guided setup making the flow explicit (audit smell M). Stages: 0 Connect to Dispatcharr (surfaces the connection/EPG-source step that must precede everything; settings still live in Settings) → 1 Sources → 2 Subscriptions → 3 EPG → 4 Channels → ▶ Generate & see results (ends on a real generate action + link to the created channels, so users get confirmation it worked). Connect + Generate are onboarding stages and cross-links, NOT new top-level nav items — nav stays the clean 4 steps.

title: Dedup + naming sweep (team filter scope, residual duplicates, copy)
description: Cross-cutting cleanup: clarify team-filter scope (currently 3 places: GlobalDefaults, EventGroupForm, Stream Ordering — audit smell E), remove any residual duplicate controls left after the moves, resolve remaining naming collisions, normalize copy/labels across the new step homes. Depends on the step homes existing.

title: Settings → system/integration only
description: After steps 2/3/4 absorb their config, strip Settings to system concerns only: Dispatcharr CONNECTION (url/creds/test + EPG source link), media servers (Emby/Jellyfin/Channels DVR), scheduled generation + channel reset, data caches, backup/restore, TSDB API key, XMLTV metadata, display/timezone. Remove emptied tabs and the Teams/Events tab-name collisions. Depends on 2/3/4.

title: ④ Channels home: absorb lifecycle, numbering, consolidation, sort priority, Dispatcharr output
description: Expand the Channels page from read-only view into the 'how/when channels are created' home. Absorb from Settings→Channels: lifecycle timing, numbering mode/range, consolidation, Sort Priority (incl. new Priority Teams), per-league channel config, stream ordering. Absorb the OUTPUT half of Settings→Dispatcharr: default channel profiles, stream profile, channel group + mode, logo cleanup (audit smell J). Keep the read-only managed-channel list as a sub-view.

title: ③ EPG home — Presentation zone (templates, names, logos, feed-separation labels)
description: Step 3 EPG home — PRESENTATION zone (the happy path). Templates (names, logos, descriptions), assignment by sport/league, feed-separation labeling. This is the creative, beginner-friendly side of 'what you want in the EPG'. The technical matching/detection side is a SEPARATE, clearly-secondary zone (vhbo.10) within the same EPG nav home so beginners don't trip over regex. One nav entry, two zones.

title: ① Sources: reframe Event Groups as stream Sources
description: Rename/reframe Event Groups as 'Sources' (step 1: groups that have streams). Make the M3U-group-as-stream-source relationship explicit (audit smell P). Mostly relabel + clarify copy + move subscription overrides out to the Subscriptions home; keep the matching/regex config pointer to step 3.

title: ② Subscriptions home: lift GlobalDefaults out of Event Groups; unify the 4 subscription locations; fold in Custom Leagues; Teams-as-mode
description: The highest-value consolidation. Create a top-level Subscriptions page that becomes the single home for 'what sports/leagues you like': move the buried GlobalDefaults composite out of the Event Groups page, absorb the league/soccer-mode controls currently scattered across Settings→Teams, Settings→Event Groups, and Settings→Channels per-league. Fold the orphaned Custom Leagues page in here (adding a league = a subscription). Team-based generation lives here as a secondary 'Followed teams' mode (this is where demoted Teams lands).

title: IA shell: 4-step nav + route structure + old→new redirects
description: Foundation for the overhaul. Replace the 7-item nav with: Dashboard, 1 Sources, 2 Subscriptions, 3 EPG/Content, 4 Channels, Settings (system). Remove Teams from top-level nav. Stand up new route paths + redirect every old URL (/teams, /templates, /event-groups, /detection-library, /custom-leagues, /epg) to its new home so bookmarks survive. Page shells can be thin at first; later beads fill them. Blocks all step-home work.

title: UI/UX Information-Architecture Overhaul (v2.7.0)
description: Reorganize the UI around the real user flow, decided with maintainer 2026-06-12. Flow: 0 Connect (Dispatcharr) → 1 Sources (groups w/ streams) → 2 Subscriptions (sports/leagues you like; Teams demoted to a mode here; Custom Leagues folded in) → 3 EPG (two zones in one nav home: Presentation/templates [happy path] + Matching/detection [tuning]) → 4 Channels (lifecycle, numbering, sort priority incl. Priority Teams, consolidation, Dispatcharr output) → ▶ Generate & see results. Nav = the clean 4 steps; Connect + Generate are onboarding bookends, not menu items. Settings stripped to system/integration only. Kills duplication (exception keywords x2, team filter x3), surfaces orphaned pages. Main introduced element for v2.7.0 alongside user-maintained leagues (eqz). Inventory: plans/ux-audit-inventory.md.

title: Spike+build: auto channel-proxy source (emulate self-ref import)
description: Channels mode should emulate the user's self-referential-import workaround: for each curated DP channel, treat the channel's OUTPUT (proxy URL) as one custom stream, so 1 channel = 1 stream and Dispatcharr manages inner failover. Validated feasible: DP streams support is_custom+url, Teamarr has POST, channel uuid available. Spike the proxy URL format, then auto-create/manage custom channel-proxy streams as candidates. Replaces the current 'pick first inner stream' logic.
design: See memory channel-proxy-source-spike.md. Proxy URL = {base}/proxy/ts/stream/{uuid} (confirmed live). Custom streams supported (url+is_custom+m3u_account). Design: auto-create 1 Teamarr-managed custom proxy stream per curated channel, lifecycle-managed, under a dedicated M3U account. Channels-mode (pick-first-stream) removed from dev as wrong abstraction. Also fix list_streams full-catalog perf bug.

title: Migration: preserve EPG-matching off-state when global switch was off (3lp1)
description: Removing the global epg_match_enabled switch (3lp1.1) changed gating: EPG matching + channel-source now activate on per-group/channel-source flags ALONE, not gated by the old global switch. Existing users who had per-group epg_match_enabled or epg_channel_source_enabled set while keeping the global switch OFF would have matching silently activate on upgrade. Migration: if old (vestigial) settings.epg_match_enabled = 0, clear epg_channel_source_enabled and all event_epg_groups.epg_match_enabled. Bump schema_version 73->74.
close_reason: Added v74 migration _migrate_v74_preserve_epg_match_offstate: if old vestigial global epg_match_enabled was OFF, clear epg_channel_source_enabled + per-group epg_match_enabled to preserve off-state across the global-switch removal. Bumped schema_version 73->74. 3 migration tests + fixed 5 version-pin assertions. Also dropped '(current)' from the stream-mode dropdown label per user. Verified on dev DB (global was ON -> correctly no-op). 856 tests pass.

Move the whole block to the Settings → Event Groups tab as a standalone Card titled 'EPG Program Matching', since EPG matching is a per-group concern. Frontend-only (Settings.tsx).

Frontend-only move (verified in browser, no duplication, EPG tab clean). Docs relocated: section moved epg.md->event-groups.md with pointer, guide location refs updated.

Now recognizes 'sports'/'sport' categories and reports is_live from the actual <live> tag. 'streams' = current _fetch_channel_source_streams behavior (one candidate per inner stream). 'channels' = group by DP channel, emit ONE representative stream per channel (e.g. primary/first stream id) tagged with the channel's EPG tvg_id, so the channel's internal stream lineup is never fragmented. Scope: GLOBAL. Touches schema.sql settings, settings/epg.py, Settings.tsx, event_group_processor.py:_fetch_channel_source_streams, tests.
close_reason: Added global epg_channel_source_mode ('streams'|'channels'). 'channels' emits one representative stream (lowest id) per DP channel in _fetch_channel_source_streams so its lineup isn't fragmented; 'streams' keeps current per-stream behavior. Cascading menu under 'Use Dispatcharr as a source'. Schema column (reconciled), full settings plumbing, 2 new tests, docs. Verified in browser + API round-trip.

Removed global gate from consumer (system-group sync, index gate, dedup path), settings plumbing (types/read/update/models/routes), and frontend toggle (un-nested buffers/channel-source/Xtream). DB column left vestigial. Docs updated (epg-matching.md, settings/epg.md, CLAUDE.md). 851 tests pass, frontend builds, verified in browser + PUT round-trip.

title: Footer easter egg: 'never support curling' line
description: Add an italic line to the app footer (MainLayout.tsx): 'Jesse, Teamarr will never support curling 🥌'. Frontend-only.
close_reason: Added italic footer line in MainLayout.tsx; verified rendering via Playwright

title: Deleting a custom league orphans its team_cache/league_cache rows
description: Lifecycle asymmetry found while verifying eqz.4: create_custom_league_and_refresh scope-caches teams into team_cache + league_cache, but delete_custom_league only removes the leagues row. The cached teams (team_cache WHERE league=code) and the league_cache row (league_slug=code) are left behind until the next FULL cache refresh wipes/rebuilds. Harmless to matching (no active league references them) but a leak. Fix: have the custom-league delete path scope-delete team_cache + league_cache for that code, mirroring _save_league_teams. Verified live 2026-06-11: deleting tst.sup left 16 team_cache rows + 1 league_cache row.
close_reason: Custom-league delete now scope-purges team_cache (WHERE league=code) + league_cache (WHERE league_slug=code) in the same transaction as the leagues-row delete, mirroring _save_league_teams. New purge_league_cache_rows() in database/leagues.py; delete_custom_league() logs purged counts. Test asserts both caches empty for the code + unrelated league untouched. 855 tests pass.

title: Sport guardrails for custom leagues
description:
A custom league's sport drives which matcher + event_type logic runs. Free-text or unsupported sports route through the wrong pipeline and silently produce broken output. Guardrails are mandatory (maintainer-flagged).

THREE GUARDRAILS:

1. SPORT ALLOWLIST (not free text). Restrict custom-league sport to the FUNCTIONALLY-SUPPORTED set, NOT the full sports table. The sports table has 17 rows but only 13 are functional (used by leagues + have matcher support): australian-football, baseball, basketball, boxing, cricket, football, hockey, lacrosse, mma, rugby, soccer, softball, volleyball. EXCLUDE tennis/golf/racing/wrestling — they are placeholder/roadmap sports (epics mf7/1tz/h31) with NO working matcher; a TSDB league under them would generate broken channels. Backend must reject a sport outside the functional allowlist (400), and the UI dropdown only offers functional sports. Source the allowlist from a single canonical place so it auto-grows when tennis/golf/etc. ship.

2. TSDB SPORT CROSS-CHECK. The test-fetch/validation step (eqz.3) must read TSDB's strSport for the entered idLeague (lookupleague.php) and confirm it maps to the user-selected sport. Reject mismatches (e.g. user picks 'soccer' but TSDB says 'Cricket') — prevents mislabeling that routes a league through the wrong matcher/event_type. Derive sport from TSDB rather than fully trusting user input.

3. EVENT_TYPE CONSISTENCY. event_type is constrained to {team_vs_team, event_card} (the only values in use). Combat/individual sports (boxing, mma) default to event_card; team sports to team_vs_team. UI should derive a sensible default by sport and constrain the choice rather than free-form.

Enforced in BOTH the write path (eqz.2) and validation endpoint (eqz.3).

title: Frontend: custom-league UI (premium-gated, test-fetch UX)
description:
UI for managing custom TSDB leagues.

Deliverables:
- Entry point (issue suggests Event Groups tab) showing a list of custom leagues (is_custom=1) with Add Custom League button + edit/delete.
- Add/edit form: league_code, provider_league_id, provider_league_name, display_name, sport, event_type, tsdb_tier. Provider is fixed to TSDB (no dropdown — TSDB-only constraint).
- PREMIUM GATE: when custom_leagues_enabled=false (no premium key), the feature is locked/hidden with a clear explainer ('Custom leagues require a TheSportsDB premium key — add one in Settings'). Non-negotiable.
- TEST-FETCH UX: a 'Test' button that calls the test-fetch endpoint and shows real sample events before the user can save. This is the core UX, not optional polish.
- Help text on how to find TSDB idLeague (provider_league_id) and exact strLeague (provider_league_name) from a thesportsdb.com league URL.
close_reason: Custom Leagues UI: dedicated page (route + Event Groups entry button, mirrors Detection Library). Premium-gated (locked card when capability.enabled=false). List table of is_custom=1 leagues with edit/delete. Add/edit dialog: code/id/name/display + sport dropdown (functional sports from capability) + event_type + tsdb_tier; league_code immutable on edit. Test-Fetch UX shows live TSDB league name/sport/sample events + name_matches + off-season warning before save; allow_empty override checkbox. Added GET /leagues/custom list endpoint + list_custom_leagues DB helper. Playwright-verified end-to-end against real backend + live TSDB: empty state, dropdown, gating, bad-id 400 toast, good-id (EPL 4328) result panel, full create->list->delete cycle (DB left clean). Frontend build clean. NOTE: premium-gate locked state not visually exercised (dev DB has a key) — covered by conditional + backend capability test. User-facing docs deferred to eqz.6.

title: Live TSDB test-fetch / validation endpoint
description:
THE bead that determines whether this feature reduces support load or increases it. The #1 failure mode (#217/#218/#219) is a wrong provider_league_name -> silent 404 / no_event_found.

Deliverables:
- Endpoint (e.g. POST /leagues/test-fetch) taking provider_league_id + provider_league_name (+ date/season), hitting TSDB live via the existing TSDB provider, returning sample events (or a clear 'TSDB returned nothing — check the exact league name' message).
- Used by the UI before save AND re-run server-side on create as a validation guardrail (block save if zero events resolve, with an override-with-warning path for genuinely off-season leagues).
- Premium-gated (403 without key).
- Surface which endpoint resolved (eventsday by name vs eventsnextleague by id) to help users debug name vs id mistakes.
close_reason: Live test-fetch validator: lookupleague(id) resolves identity, validate_tsdb_sport_matches cross-checks sport, eventsnextleague(id) returns sample events. Premium-gated (403). Wired into create_custom_league as the authoritative server-side guardrail — blocks save on zero events with allow_empty override for off-season. name_matches flag (typed name vs canonical strLeague) + resolved_via surface the id-vs-name debugging the bead wanted. SCOPING: did not add a literal eventsday-by-name fallback path; the id-based path is reliable and name_matches covers the debugging need — can be a follow-up if real id/name confusion shows up. Raw TSDB client methods (lookup_league_raw/get_events_by_date_raw/get_next_events_raw) added uncached. Tests cover gate, unresolvable id, sport mismatch, empty-ok, key passthrough, create guardrail + override + cross-check.

title: League write service + CRUD routes (TSDB-only, is_custom flag)
description:
Add the write path the leagues table currently lacks (database/leagues.py is read-only from the frontend today).

Deliverables:
- Add is_custom INTEGER DEFAULT 0 column to leagues table in schema.sql (reconciliation auto-adds on upgrade; no migration block needed per CLAUDE.md). Distinguishes user rows from built-ins.
- Service methods in a service/consumer layer (NOT in the route — CLAUDE.md layer rule): create_custom_league / update_custom_league / delete_custom_league.
- Routes POST /leagues, PUT /leagues/{code}, DELETE /leagues/{code}.
- HARD CONSTRAINTS: reject any provider != 'tsdb' (400). Edits/deletes allowed ONLY on rows where is_custom=1 (404/403 otherwise) so built-ins can never be mutated/deleted via API. All routes 403 when no premium key (from gate bead).
- Required fields: league_code, provider_league_id (TSDB numeric idLeague), provider_league_name (exact strLeague), display_name, sport, event_type; tsdb_tier optional.
- Guard against colliding with a built-in league_code on create.
- Tests for: non-tsdb rejection, built-in mutation rejection, premium gate, restart survival.
close_reason: Write path: is_custom column + DB helpers (insert/update/delete custom rows, all guarded by is_custom=1) + service create/update/delete with full policy (premium gate, TSDB-only, sport/event_type guardrails, code-collision guard, built-in protection 404/403) + POST/PUT/DELETE routes. Restart-survival test confirms novel codes persist across schema re-seed. 55 tests.

title: Premium-gate capability check (backend + shared signal)
description:
Foundational. Single source of truth for 'can this user use custom leagues': settings.tsdb_api_key is set/non-empty (get_tsdb_api_key in teamarr/database/settings/read.py:235). This presence is what makes the TSDB client premium (providers/__init__.py:80 -> client.is_premium).

Deliverables:
- A backend capability flag (e.g. extend an existing settings/capabilities GET response, or add GET /leagues/custom/capability) returning { custom_leagues_enabled: bool } driven solely by premium-key presence.
- ALL custom-league write routes (create/edit/delete/test-fetch) must hard-check this and return 403 when no premium key. Gate lives in the service layer, not just the UI.
- No new column needed for the gate itself.

title: [epic] Custom Leagues via UI (TSDB-only, premium-gated)
description:
Closes #230. Let users add custom leagues through the UI with zero code changes and no redeployment, retiring the manual league-request loop (#220-229 are all TSDB soccer).

NON-NEGOTIABLE CONSTRAINTS (per maintainer):
1. TSDB provider ONLY. Reject any non-tsdb provider at the API layer. ESPN/hockeytech/mlbstats/supabase are out of scope — they have path-style ids, auto-discovery, or provider-specific quirks.
2. Feature is HARD-GATED behind a TSDB premium key. The entire feature (UI + write routes) is locked unless settings.tsdb_api_key is set. Rationale: TSDB free tier is unreliable for arbitrary leagues (eventsnextleague returns ~5 events/day, lookupteam broken, lookuptable limited to featured soccer) — gating on premium avoids turning every custom league into a support ticket.

ARCHITECTURE (already verified feasible):
- League system is fully DB-driven; TSDB provider reads provider_league_id + provider_league_name straight off the leagues row (tsdb/client.py:394-423). No hardcoded league lists.
- Restart-safe: novel league_code rows survive (CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS no-op; single INSERT OR REPLACE only touches built-in codes; reconciliation only ADDs columns; the lone DELETE FROM leagues is a one-shot v73 migration on hardcoded MiLB codes).
- Premium gate signal: get_tsdb_api_key(conn) non-empty -> client.is_premium True.

THE REAL RISK: the #1 failure mode across #217/#218/#219 is a wrong provider_league_name -> silent 404 / no_event_found. A custom-league form that just writes a row moves the support burden from 'file an issue' to 'why is my league empty' — worse, because it looks like a Teamarr bug. The MVP's center of gravity MUST be a live test-fetch against TSDB before save.

title: #219: TSDB match fails — no_event_found despite reverse-alias resolving league candidates
description:
Reported in #219 (v2.6.0). TSDB-sourced soccer leagues (swe.3.n / swe.3.s 'Ettan'). Teams classify correctly, REVERSE_ALIAS resolves team1/team2 to the right league candidates ({'swe.3.n'}), but TeamMatcher returns [FAILED] reason=no_event_found and 0/4 matched.

Key clue from reporter: team alias rows are stored with provider='espn' even though the teams only exist via the TSDB provider (consistent across other TSDB leagues incl. uru.2). TSDB /eventsday.php?l=4674 confirms events ARE available for the date with matching idHomeTeam/idAwayTeam. So events exist and aliases resolve, but the event lookup keyed by provider likely mismatches (espn alias provider vs tsdb event provider). Investigate provider-keying in the event index / alias storage path so TSDB league aliases carry provider=tsdb (or make event lookup provider-agnostic within a league).
notes: ROOT CAUSE (verified by code trace): swe.3.n/swe.3.s are hand-inserted custom leagues — NOT in schema.sql (zero Swedish leagues) and no UI/API exists to add custom leagues. Chain: (1) custom league can't be in a group's subscription via UI → is_group_league=False in matcher._prefetch_events. (2) matcher.py:491 forces cache_only=True for TSDB when not is_group_league. (3) nothing warms that cache — prewarm_tsdb_leagues only runs in the TEAM path (team_processor.py:327), never event groups. (4) so swe.3.n events never enter all_events; the events present (no_event_found log at team_matcher.py:1001 fires only when list non-empty) come from other known leagues. (5) reverse-alias candidate filter checks against search_leagues=ALL known leagues (matcher.py:801), so it passes swe.3.n, then filters all_events to swe.3.n → empty → 0 matched. provider='espn' on aliases is a RED HERRING — matcher keys aliases by (alias,league) only, never reads alias.provider. NOT a matcher logic bug. Latent separate gap: non-subscribed TSDB leagues in event groups never get event-cache warmed (by design best-effort), but official TSDB leagues work fine when subscribed (cache_only=False → API fetch).
close_reason: Not a bug — unsupported hand-added custom leagues (swe.3.n/swe.3.s). Verified not in schema.sql/dev DB/docs/code; only Svenska Cupen ships. Custom leagues require code changes in multiple places; directed reporter to submit a league add request. GH #219 closed not-planned.

title: Export/import stream ordering settings
description: Add a way to export the current stream ordering rules to a JSON file and import a rules file (full replacement). Purely UI-driven, reusing existing GET/PUT /settings/stream-ordering endpoints and modeling the export UX on the existing template export pattern.

title: Cascading DP-group multi-select under 'Use DP as source' toggle (frontend)

Under the 'Use Dispatcharr channels as an EPG source' toggle (Settings.tsx ~2730), when ON reveal a multi-select picker 'Select Dispatcharr Groups to include' populated by the new available-DP-groups endpoint. Save selection to epg_channel_source_groups. Label: 'Leave empty to include all groups (slower).' Reuse CheckboxListPicker (searchable multi-select) per memory. This same selection drives the sorting dropdown in the sibling bead.
